message:
fix(pager,wal,mvcc): consolidate page-1 header patching; add WAL frame gauge; harden writer lock

Pager: Move page-1 header patching (page_count, change_counter,
version_valid_for) out of the commit functions and into
SimpleTransaction::commit where the write_set is still mutable. This
eliminates duplicate patching code in both journal and WAL commit paths
and avoids an extra disk read-modify-write cycle in journal mode.

WAL metrics: Add wal_frames_current gauge that tracks the live
WalFile::frame_count. Updated on create, open, append, batch_append,
and reset. Exposed in WalMetricsSnapshot and its Display impl. This
gives the checkpoint advisor a live estimate of WAL backlog size.

WAL index: Fix hash insert doc comment — every appended frame now gets
a unique hash slot even if the same page was previously written, which
is necessary for readers to find the correct version relative to their
read mark.

MVCC/BOCPD: Fix changepoint probability calculation — the prior
predictive probability for the new run (r_t=0) was incorrectly using
each existing run's predictive, rather than the fresh-prior predictive.
Now uses NormalGammaStats::new() / BetaBinomialStats::new() for the
changepoint entry.

MVCC/SHM: Replace separate serialized_writer_pid (u32) and alignment
padding with a packed pid_and_gen (u64) that incorporates a monotonic
generation counter in the upper 32 bits. This makes the CAS in
release_serialized_writer immune to the ABA problem when the same PID
re-acquires the lock before the previous holder's release CAS fires.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
